summary:
Accessory canals frequently emerge from the mesial part of mandibular molar roots and are less likely to appear equally distributed across all sides. Thus, option B represents a more accurate location based on this anatomical pattern.
To refine it further while keeping within two concise sentences:

Accessories typically form near the mesial side of mandibular molars' crowns; hence they most probably stem from there as opposed to other locations like lateral or bilateral areas. Consequently, \(\boxed{\text{option } B}\) aligns with common dental observations regarding root morphology and canal variations. Summary focusing only on essential details:

Accessory canals usually develop close to the mesial area of crown-root junction in mandibular molars, making them least probable elsewhere. Hence, \(\boxed{\text{option } B}\). The summary emphasizes that accessories predominantly occur mesially without specifying exact measurements but highlighting their frequent appearance relative to specific tooth structures.
